input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display is enforced by default, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated in Android API 30 and removed in API 35.

The solution in .NET 10 / Android 15 is to use the new `KeyboardInsets` approach or handle `WindowInsets` properly. Specifically, in .NET MAUI for .NET 10, you should use the `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in combination with proper inset handling, or use the new MAUI APIs.
 
In Android 15, the old `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` flag no longer works because edge-to-edge is now mandatory, so the modern approach is to listen to `WindowInsets` changes—particularly the IME insets—and adjust the layout padding dynamically. For .NET MAUI specifically, the team has been working on better keyboard handling, so I should check what the current recommended approach is for .NET 10. - On Android: Use `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` to detect keyboard insets and adjust your layout padding accordingly.
- The `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust` enum was marked obsolete/removed.

For a chat screen in .NET MAUI with .NET 10, the most practical approach is keeping `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the AndroidManifest.xml while handling edge-to-edge insets properly using the insets listener.

Since Android 15 enforces edge-to-edge layouts and ignores the old `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` behavior, I need to use WindowInsets to detect when the IME appears and apply padding to the content accordingly. Setting up the insets listener in the Android platform code...
